Marcela Espinosa
CRM Enterprise Integrations Workflow Optimization Automation

Trust Center Enhancements

Turning a compliance document portal into an automated, insight-driven sales and security tool — through three interconnected capabilities that removed friction at every layer of the access workflow.

Senior Product Designer
Multi-capability feature initiative
Overview

Three capabilities. One shift — from reactive portal to proactive platform.

Drata's Trust Center allows companies to securely share compliance documentation with prospective customers during security reviews and sales cycles. As adoption grew, customers began encountering friction managing document access requests and demonstrating the value of their Trust Center to internal stakeholders.

This initiative focused on improving the Trust Center experience through three complementary capabilities:

  • Salesforce integration to automate access request approvals and surface CRM context during reviews
  • NDA automation to reduce redundant agreement workflows
  • Trust Center analytics to provide visibility into document usage and engagement

Together, these improvements streamlined access management for Trust Center administrators while giving organizations better insight into how Trust Center contributes to their sales and security workflows.

My Role

Leading design across three interconnected layers of a complex workflow.

I led product design for three interconnected Trust Center capabilities — each targeting a distinct layer of friction in how organizations manage and demonstrate compliance during security reviews and sales cycles.

My responsibilities included:

  • Designing the Salesforce integration experience used to configure and manage Trust Center access workflows
  • Defining how CRM data would surface within document access requests to support faster decision-making
  • Designing automation flows that streamline request approvals and reduce redundant NDA processes
  • Designing the Trust Center analytics dashboard to provide visibility into document usage and engagement
  • Partnering with product and engineering teams to translate workflow requirements into implementable system behaviors
Impact

From manual and reactive to automated, informed, and insight-driven.

This work shifted Trust Center from a manual, reactive workflow into a proactive, automated system — giving organizations the visibility they needed to understand and demonstrate the value of their compliance investment.

Key outcomes:

Single informed decision point

Replaced the manual verification loop — account, opportunity, and contact data from Salesforce surface directly inside each access request, eliminating the need to switch tools

Redundant NDA step eliminated

Eliminated the redundant NDA step for prospects who had already signed — reducing friction at a moment that directly affects sales velocity

First real business visibility

Gave organizations their first real view into how Trust Center drives business — surfacing page visits, document downloads, access request status, opportunity stage distribution, and influenced revenue

Product repositioning

Repositioned Trust Center from a static compliance portal into a tool that actively supports both security reviews and sales cycles — a meaningful shift in how the product creates value

The Problem

Every access request required manual work — and none of it was visible.

Trust Center administrators frequently receive document access requests from prospective customers during security reviews.

However, managing these requests often required manual verification steps:

  • Determining whether the requester was an actual prospect
  • Confirming whether an NDA was already signed
  • Reviewing each request individually before granting access

At the same time, administrators lacked visibility into how their Trust Center was being used.

Many customers wanted to understand:

  • Which documents were being requested most frequently
  • How often their Trust Center was being visited
  • Whether Trust Center activity influenced sales cycles

Without automation or analytics, managing document access and demonstrating Trust Center value required significant manual effort.

Streamlining Access Decisions with Salesforce

CRM context at the moment of decision — without leaving the platform.

To streamline access request workflows, we introduced a Salesforce integration that allows Trust Center administrators to connect their CRM data to the platform.

The integration allows Trust Center to retrieve relevant CRM information when document access requests are submitted.

When a request is made, Trust Center can:

  • Look up the requester's contact information in Salesforce
  • Retrieve associated account and opportunity data
  • Identify internal sales contacts related to the request

This information is surfaced directly in the access request interface, allowing administrators to quickly verify whether a requester is associated with a legitimate sales opportunity.

This reduces the need for administrators to manually search CRM systems before approving document access.

Click to zoom · drag to pan

Access request panel with Salesforce CRM context — contact, account, opportunity, and NDA status surfaced inline

Eliminating Redundant NDA Workflows

If they already signed, they shouldn't have to sign again.

Another common challenge was managing NDA requirements.

Prospective customers requesting Trust Center documents were often required to sign an NDA before gaining access — even if they had already signed one with the company.

To address this, we designed automation rules that allow Trust Center to check Salesforce records for existing NDAs.

If a valid agreement is found, the platform can automatically bypass the NDA requirement and proceed with the access request flow.

This eliminates redundant agreements and significantly reduces friction for prospective customers requesting documentation.

Click to zoom · drag to pan

NDA bypass configuration — automation rules and access request flow with NDA status

Introducing Trust Center Analytics

Giving organizations the language to talk about Trust Center in business terms.

While automation improved the operational workflow, administrators still lacked insight into how their Trust Center was being used.

To address this, I designed a Trust Center analytics dashboard that provides visibility into key usage metrics.

The dashboard allows administrators to track:

  • Total document downloads over time
  • Number of access requests received
  • Trust Center page visits
  • Engagement with specific documents
  • Number of requests on each opportunity stage from their CRM
  • Revenue of opportunities that requested documents

These insights help organizations understand how Trust Center contributes to security reviews, customer trust, and sales processes.

The analytics data can also be exported for reporting and internal presentations.

Click to zoom · drag to pan

Trust Center analytics dashboard — usage metrics, opportunity stage distribution, and influenced revenue

Reflection

The most impactful work happened at the boundaries between systems.

This project highlighted how operational friction often emerges at the intersection of security workflows and business processes. Sometimes the most impactful design work isn't always about the interface — it's about understanding where workflows break down across system boundaries.

By designing automation that bridged those systems, we removed the cognitive load of context-switching during a high-stakes decision. And by surfacing analytics, we gave teams the language to talk about Trust Center's value in business terms, not just compliance ones.

Adding analytics further strengthened the product by helping organizations quantify the value of their Trust Center and better understand how customers interact with their compliance documentation.

Together, these improvements transformed Trust Center from a static document repository into a more automated and insight-driven platform supporting both security and sales teams.
